summary: DHH announces that approximately 4,700 copies of 'Agile Web Development with Rails' shipped in a single week after a prolonged shortage from the previous printing. The book had been out of stock for a while, leaving buyers at events like JAOO empty-handed. The new shipment went to direct customers, Amazon, and local bookstores. He reflects on the impressive number with a lighthearted tone.
key_points: 4,700 copies of Agile Web Development with Rails shipped in one week after a supply shortage
Demand had built up significantly during the out-of-stock period, with even conference attendees unable to buy copies
The Rails book's commercial success signals strong early adoption of the framework in 2005
money_quotes: Boy, there was a lot of pent up demand after running short on the last printing.
They didn't even have any copies of the book at JAOO — despite having lots of willing buyers. What a shame.
4,700 copies in one week. Glad I didn't have to lick the stamps on all of those.
key_insight: The rapid sellout and massive restock of the Rails book demonstrated the explosive early demand for Ruby on Rails in 2005.
